mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate sustained above its trailing twelve-month average signals restrictive monetary policy that raises the equity risk premium and compresses valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 55% of months when FEDFUNDS is above its 12-month trailing average, or the Rank IC is positive over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1900 thousand units signal a contraction in residential construction and related consumer wealth effects that reduce aggregate demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are negative in fewer than 50% of triggered instances when HOUST is below 1900 over any 2-year window, or the strategy Sharpe ratio is not significantly below zero.

mechanism_narrative: Joint weakness in payroll employment and a rising unemployment rate indicate deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce household income growth and corporate revenue expectations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of falling PAYEMS and rising UNRATE fails to produce negative 5-day forward returns in more than 50% of occurrences, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power in a 3-year rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: During elevated inflation, month-over-month declines in real retail sales signal demand contraction that pressures corporate profit margins, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL year-over-year exceeds 3%, declining RSAFS does not precede negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production signals a contraction in manufacturing output and capacity utilization that reduces earnings visibility for industrial sectors, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month declines in INDPRO are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of triggered months over any 2-year window, or directional accuracy is not statistically different from random (binomial p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a high federal funds rate and declining housing starts tightens financial conditions and chokes off credit-sensitive spending, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between FEDFUNDS and HOUST does not significantly predict negative 5-day returns over a 3-year rolling window, or the signal underperforms a univariate FEDFUNDS model.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production above its six-month trend signals improving supply-side efficiency and corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Positive deviations in INDPRO from its 6-month trend are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations over a 3-year rolling window, or the Rank IC is negative and statistically significant.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate rising above 5.0 percent triggers a repricing of earnings expectations as labor cost pressures and demand fears intensify, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: UNRATE crossings above 5.0% are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in fewer than 55% of instances over any 3-year rolling window, or the average forward return is not significantly negative.
